prompt: Baseline on '{dataset}' ({task}, metric {metric}): accuracy {accuracy} at epoch {epoch}.
Grouping rule for train_setup, by strategy_type:
n_group -> slice named_parameters() into contiguous depth groups sized by the split ratios.
cyclic  -> bucket named_parameters() by index modulo len(multipliers) (interleaved, not depth-contiguous).
Each group's lr = prm['lr'] * its multiplier. Pass the groups to the optimizer instead of self.parameters().

Build the groups INLINE, directly inside train_setup, as local variables. Do NOT define or call any new method (no self.groups(), no self.get_param_groups(), no helper functions) — every name you introduce must be a local variable built and used within this same train_setup body.

Worked example (2 equal groups, multipliers [0.1, 1.0]) — match this STYLE only. The @@ line/counts and context below belong to a DIFFERENT, hypothetical file and WILL be wrong for your baseline. Derive your own hunk from the actual <nn>{nn_code}</nn> given below: copy its real train_setup context character-for-character (including any blank line before 'def learn'), use its real line number, and set old_count = the unchanged+removed lines you wrote, new_count = the unchanged+added lines you wrote — never reuse the numbers 19/6/19/17 shown here.

Copy the baseline's train_setup context EXACTLY (names, indentation, the self.optimizer=... line) so the diff applies cleanly.
Touch ONLY train_setup — never __init__, forward, or learn. Use self.device (from __init__), never prm['device'].
supported_hyperparameters() must list only the prm keys actually used.
Output exactly ONE <hp>, ONE <tr>, and ONE <delta> block — never repeat a tag.
